sanitization: Consumer names and update mechanisms only. No credential values.
completeness_reason: Repository-side consumers are fully enumerated from source. Provider-side consumer state (Supabase Edge Function Secrets contents, Vercel project environment variables, GitHub Actions repository secrets, VPS systemd unit environment, Cloudflare tunnel configuration) could NOT be enumerated from this environment: no authenticated provider CLI or write-capable API session is reachable. Several consumers therefore remain UNKNOWN, which by section 5 of the loop specification forbids starting any rotation.

consumers.consumer: Supabase Edge Functions runtime (50 ACTIVE functions)
consumers.note: Supabase injects SUPABASE_URL/ANON/SERVICE_ROLE automatically; must NOT be set as an explicit edge secret.

consumers.consumer: root ops scripts (reset_users*, update_passwords*, query_db.cjs, create_users_only.cjs, delete_db_users.js, check_*.cjs)

consumers.status: UNKNOWN — workflow declares no secrets.* references; whether repository secrets exist could not be verified without GitHub API access.

map.positive_validation: Authenticated read of a low-cardinality metadata table; confirm RLS bypass still functions.
map.negative_validation: Old key must return 401 invalid API key on the same endpoint.
map.rollback: Legacy JWT-based service-role keys cannot be un-rotated once the JWT secret is reset; if using the modern sb_secret_ model, re-enable the previous key in the dashboard.
map.expected_downtime: Zero if the modern dual-key model is in use; total edge outage if the legacy JWT secret is reset without staged updates.

consumers.consumer: root ops scripts using direct Postgres connections
any external analytics/BI or pooler client

map.positive_validation: SELECT 1 over the session pooler with the new password.
map.negative_validation: Connection attempt with the old password must be rejected with an authentication failure.
map.rollback: Reset the password again in the Supabase dashboard; no overlap window exists — the change is immediate and global.
map.expected_downtime: Momentary for every direct-Postgres consumer; edge functions are unaffected (they use PostgREST, not DATABASE_URL).
blocking_unknowns: Whether any unmanaged external client holds this credential

consumers.consumer: Supabase CLI on the operator workstation

map.positive_validation: List projects with the new token.
map.negative_validation: Same call with the old token must return 401.
map.rollback: Issue an additional token; PATs support overlap.

map.positive_validation: Authorized internal call returns 200 with the new shared secret.
map.negative_validation: Same call with the old shared secret must return 401/403.
map.rollback: Re-set the previous shared secret in edge secrets; caller and callee must be updated together — no overlap.
map.expected_downtime: Ingestion/pipeline gap for the duration of the caller/callee update skew.
